phrases.intent_en: Ask where the cheese counter is
phrases.text_local: Excusez-moi, où se trouve le rayon fromage, s'il vous plaît ?
phrases.pronunciation: ex-kyoo-ZAY-mwah, oo suh TROOV luh ray-YON froh-MAHJ, seel voo PLAY
phrases.cultural_note: French speakers take cheese very seriously — asking for the fromage counter signals real culinary interest and will often earn an enthusiastic response.

responses.french: Le rayon fromage est au fond, à gauche.
responses.english: The cheese counter is at the back, on the left.
responses.phonetic: luh ray-YON froh-MAHJ ay oh FON, ah GOHSH

phrases.text_local: Est-ce que ce produit est biologique ?
phrases.pronunciation: ess kuh suh pro-DWEE ay bee-oh-loh-ZHEEK
phrases.cultural_note: The word 'biologique' (or just 'bio') is the standard French term for organic. The 'bio' label in France is regulated under the EU organic certification — French shoppers use it casually.

phrases.intent_en: Order a sandwich at the deli counter
phrases.text_local: Je voudrais un sandwich au jambon et au fromage, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: juh voo-DRAY un SOND-weech oh jhom-BON ay oh froh-MAHJ, seel voo PLAY
phrases.cultural_note: At a French deli counter (charcuterie), it is polite to greet the staff before ordering. 'Je voudrais' (I would like) is preferred over 'Je veux' (I want), which sounds abrupt.

responses.french: Bien sûr. Vous voulez quel pain ?
responses.english: Of course. What bread would you like?
responses.phonetic: byan SOOR. voo voo-LAY kel PAN

phrases.text_local: Avez-vous des produits locaux du Kentucky ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah-VAY-voo day pro-DWEE lo-KOH doo ken-TAH-kee
phrases.cultural_note: Co-ops in France ('coopératives') strongly emphasize local sourcing ('circuits courts'). Asking about local products fits right into this shared value and usually sparks a proud response from staff.

responses.french: Oui, nous avons du miel et des confitures locales.
responses.english: Yes, we have local honey and jams.
responses.phonetic: wee, noo ZAH-von doo myel ay day kon-fee-TOOR lo-KAL

responses.french: Regardez ce rayon — tout vient de la région.
responses.english: Look at this shelf — everything comes from the region.
responses.phonetic: ruh-gar-DAY suh ray-YON — too vyan duh lah ray-ZHYON

phrases.intent_en: Ask the price of an item
phrases.text_local: Combien coûte ceci, s'il vous plaît ?
phrases.pronunciation: kom-BYAN koot suh-SEE, seel voo PLAY
phrases.cultural_note: In French markets, prices are usually posted, but asking is perfectly normal. Pointing at the item while asking is acceptable and helps avoid ambiguity.

responses.french: Le prix est affiché sur l'étiquette.
responses.english: The price is shown on the label.
responses.phonetic: luh pree ay ta-fee-SHAY soor lay-tee-KET

phrases.text_local: Vous avez un très beau choix de produits !
phrases.pronunciation: voo za-VAY un tray boh SHWAH duh pro-DWEE
phrases.cultural_note: Complimenting a shop's selection is warmly received in France and builds rapport. French shopkeepers take pride in curating quality products and appreciate customers who notice.
